visitorToStudentPara: Transitioning from visitor status to student status (also known as a change of status) can be an option for you if you have been staying in Canada as a visitor and wish to pursue full-time studies in a recognized academic institution. This program, known as the Visitor to Student Program (VTS), allows you to extend your stay and pursue studies at a designated learning institution (DLI). This process involves you applying for a student visa or changing your current visa to a student visa.
BenifitsHeading: Benefits of Changing Your Status from Visitor to Student
BenifitsSubHead: The Visitor to Student Permit Program offers several benefits for international students, including:
b1: A Guaranteed Investment Certificate (GIC) is not required to apply for a Study Permit when you apply from inside Canada.
b2: You do not need to pay a 1-year fee to college or university, upfront.
b3: While you study, you are allowed to work up to 24 hours per week, starting September, 2024.
b4: Every 2 semesters, you are allowed to take a scheduled break for one semester. During this time, there are no restrictions on your work hours.
b5: If you decide to stay in Canada after completing your studies, you may be eligible for a post-graduate work permit, which can give you valuable work experience and enhance your chances of obtaining permanent residency.
b6: You can continue your studies without having to return to your home country.
b7: You can avoid the hassle and expense of traveling back and forth between Canada and your home country.
EligibilityHeading: Eligibility Criteria for the Student Permit Program
e1: You are already in Canada legally with a valid status, such as a valid study permit, work permit, or visitor record.
e2: You have taken a prerequisite course inside Canada.
e3: You are enrolled in a full-time program at a designated learning institution (DLI).
e4: You have sufficient funds to support yourself while you study in Canada, including tuition, living expenses, and return transportation..
e6: You have a valid Provincial Attestation Letter or PAL.
EligibilityListHead: In addition, you should meet at least one of the following conditions:
UkrainHeading: you’re a Ukrainian national or family member of a Ukrainian national
u1: you have a valid study or work permit
u2: your spouse, common-law partner or parent has a valid study or work permit
u3: you’re a minor child in primary or secondary school
u4: you’re an exchange student or visiting student
u5: you completed a short-term course or study program required to be accepted at a DLI
u6: you or your spouse, common-law partner or dependent child has a temporary resident permit (TRP) valid for 6 months or more
u7: you’re being sponsored to immigrate and you already applied for permanent residence (if you’re eligible)
u8: you or your spouse, common-law partner or dependent child are subject to an unenforceable removal order
SpouseDepenChildHeading: you’re the spouse, common-law partner or dependent child of:
sdc1: an athlete on a team based in Canada
sdc4: military personnel on duty in Canada or
sdc5SubLi1: you’re a refugee claimant in Canada or a family member of a refugee claimant in Canada
HowtoApplyHeading: How to Apply for a Study Permit When Applying as a Visitor
HowtoApplySubHead: You will need to provide the same documents as you would if you were applying from outside Canada. This includes your passport, letter of acceptance from your DLI, proof of financial support, and police certificates. Here are 5 easy steps we help you follow to process your application:
ha4: Get your documents and submit your application.
RefusalHeading: Reasons for Refusals of Study Permit When Applying as a Visitor
RefusalSubHead: You should have a Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from a DLI. If you do not have a LOA, your application will be refused.
r1: You do not have a Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from a DLI.
r2: If you do not have strong ties to your home country, such as a job offer or property ownership.
r3: You do not have enough money to cover tuition, living expenses, and return transportation.
r4: You provided false or misleading information on your application.
r5: You have a history of immigration violations, such as overstaying a visa or working without authorization.
r6: You have a criminal record, depending on the nature of the crime.
r7: You submitted incomplete or incorrect documentation with your application.
r8: You did not provide sufficient evidence to show your genuine intent to study.
s1: If you have faced a refusal for any of the reasons mentioned above, don't worry. With over 10 years of experience, we specialize in handling previously refused cases. We have successfully secured approval for students who have had multiple refusals, long gaps in education, and are of mature age.
s2: We achieve this by tailoring our approach to your specific case and addressing each concern raised by the officer in previous refusals. Using case law, we identify similar cases with positive outcomes and apply them as precedents. This is why we have a high success rate, with over 90% approval in such cases. At Brightlight Immigration, we have a dedicated team of visa application specialists ready to assist you from the start of the application process to obtaining your visa. Start your process now.
IfYouSubmitHeading: If you submit your study permit application on or after November 1, 2024
IfYouSubmitSubHead: NOTE: Changes were Announced to the Post Graduate Work Permit (PGWP) Eligibility - September 18, 2024
IfYouSubmitPara: If you submit your study permit application on or after November 1, 2024, you must also meet the new requirements that apply to your situation to be eligible to apply for a PGWP.
IfYouGraduatedHeading: If you Graduated from a University Bachelor’s degree, Master’s Degree or Doctoral Degree Program:
IfYouGraduatedLi1: Language requirement: You must prove your English or French language skills with a minimum level of Canadian Language Benchmarks (CLB) 7 in English or Niveaux de competence linguistique canadiens (NCLC) 7 in French in all 4 language areas.
IfYouGraduatedLi2: Field of study requirement: All fields of study are eligible. There is no additional field of study requirement.
IfYouGraduatedAnotherHeading: If you Graduated from Any Other University Program:
IfYouGradAnothLi1: Language requirement: You must prove your English or French language skills with a minimum level of CLB 7 in English or NCLC 7 in French in all 4 language areas.
IfYouGradAnothLi2: Field of study requirement:You must graduate in an eligible field of study.
IfYouGraduatedCollegeHeading: If you graduated from a College Program or Any Other Program Not listed Above:
IfYouGradCollLi1: Language requirement: You must prove your English or French language skills with a minimum level of CLB 5 in English or NCLC 5 in French in all 4 language areas.
IfYouGradCollLi2: Field of study requirement:You must graduate in an eligible field of study.
FieldStudyRequPara: If your study program has a field of study requirement, you must graduate from a program linked to certain occupations in long-term shortage. The fields of study are divided into 5 broad categories:
ipn1: To apply for a study permit as a visitor in Canada, you must submit your completion letter for the prerequisite program along with your letter of acceptance.
ipn2: Ensure that your acceptance letter clearly states that the program is a prerequisite for admission.
ipn3: If the post-secondary school you wish to attend is not on the DLI List, you cannot apply for a study permit.
ipn4: September 18, 2024. Make sure you are choosing a course that is eligible.
ipn5: During the prerequisite program, you're not eligible to work off-campus. However, once you've completed the prerequisite and commenced your main program, you can work off-campus.

q1: What is the Visitor to Student Permit Program?
qa1: The Visitor to Student Permit Program allows international students who are already in Canada on a valid visitor visa or Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) to change their status to a study permit and continue their studies here.
q2: Who is eligible for the Visitor to Student Permit Program?
qa2:
To be eligible for the Visitor to Student Permit Program, you must meet the following criteria:
  1. Be physically present in Canada on a valid visitor visa or eTA at the time of applying for the VTS.
  2. Have received a Letter Of Acceptance (LOA) from a designated learning institution (DLI) in Canada.
  3. Have sufficient financial resources to cover your tuition, living expenses, and return transportation.
q3: What are the benefits of the Visitor to Student Permit Program?
qa3:
To be eligible for the Visitor to Student Permit Program, you must meet the following criteria:
  1. Continuing your studies without having to return to your home country.
  2. Avoid the hassle and expense of traveling back and forth between Canada and your home country.
  3. If you decide to stay in Canada after completing your studies, you may be eligible for a post-graduate work permit, which can give you valuable work experience and enhance your chances of obtaining permanent residency.
q4: How long does the Visitor to Student Permit Program application process typically take?
qa4: The processing time for the Visitor to Student Permit Program application varies depending on the individual circumstances of the applicant. However, it typically takes between 4-8 weeks for IRCC to make a decision.
q5: What are the fees associated with the Visitor to Student Permit Program application?
qa5:
There are two fees associated with the Visitor to Student Permit Program application: To be eligible for the Visitor to Student Permit Program, you must meet the following criteria:
  1. Study permit application fee: This fee is CAD$150 for adults and CAD$75 for children under 18.
  2. Processing fee: This fee is CAD$200 for adults and CAD$100 for children under 18.
q6: Do I need to pay full-year tuition fees, when I am applying from inside Canada Study Permit?
qa6: You do not need to pay a 1-year fee to college or university, upfront. In most cases, you will only pay 1 semester fee to get a Letter of Acceptance (LOA).
q7: Do I need a Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) when you apply for a Study Permit from inside Canada?
qa7:
No, you do not need a Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) when you apply for a Study Permit from inside Canada.
 .
q8: Is there any age requirement to apply for study permit inside Canada?
qa8: Also, if you have your spouse who has a valid study or work permit inside Canada, you can still apply for a study permit inside Canada. The answer to that question is no, there is no such age limit. We have students who are in their 20s and we have students who are in their 50s, so everybody can apply.
q9: If you have an education gap can I still apply for study permit inside Canada?
qa9: The answer is yes. Even if you have an education gap, there are a lot of institutions in Canada which would still accept you, so it's not a problem. We receive this question from a lot of people that what are the financial requirements if you want to apply a study permit inside Canada.
q10: What is the financial requirements or proof of funds (POF) for applying for study permit inside Canada ?
qa10:
So this financial requirement remains same as somebody who's applying overseas or even if they're applying inside Canada. You need to have access to at least one year tuition fee which are going to pay to a public college or a private college and you also need to have access to the living expenses for at least one year of tuition of your state.
If you have any dependents like spouse or kids, you need to provide additional funding for them as well.
q11: Is it better to apply for study permit within Canada or outside Canada?
qa11: It's generally better to apply for a study permit within Canada because the eligibility and financial requirements are more lenient, and the success rate is higher. You won't need to take English proficiency tests like IELTS, CELPIP, or PTE. There's no requirement for a Provincial attestation letter; only a letter of acceptance is necessary. Additionally, you don't need to purchase a Guaranteed Investment Certificate (GIC), and you can obtain your study permit without leaving Canada. This option also accommodates acceptable gaps between employment and education. Moreover, you only need to pay fees for one semester instead of a full year, as required when applying from outside Canada. This route is particularly advantageous for those unable to apply in their home country or with a history of previous refusals.
q12: Do I need to take IELTS/CELPIP/PTE for admission?
qa12: No, you do not need to provide English language proficiency test scores if you complete a short-term English language course in Canada.
q13: How long is the prerequisite program?
qa13: It can range from 4-16 weeks, depending upon your profile and program selection.
q14: Do I need to purchase GIC for study permit application?
qa14: If you're applying for a study permit from inside Canada, you typically do not need to purchase a Guaranteed Investment Certificate (GIC).  If you're already in Canada, you should focus on meeting the financial proof requirements relevant to your specific situation, such as bank statements, proof of tuition payment of one semester, or other documentation showing you have adequate funds to support yourself during your studies.
q15: Can my spouse and kids stay with me during the process?
qa15: Yes, we will assist you in submitting their individual applications to stay longer in Canada.
q16: How long is the study permit valid when it is converted from a visitor visa?
qa16: The duration of study permit depends on the length of your program. You will also be allowed to live for a few more weeks after your program completion.
q17: If the six-month validity period of my visitor stay is about to expire and I have already submitted my study permit application, do I need to leave the country?
qa17: No, you do not need to leave the country. Once you have submitted your study permit application, your status in Canada will be changed to “Maintained Status” until you receive the decision on your study permit application.
q18: Will I get a Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) after completing the program?
qa18: You will be able to obtain PGWP if your program meets the PGWP requirements as per IRCC and maintains full-time student status throughout your studies.
q19: Do I need PAL if I apply for study permit from inside Canada
qa19: Yes, you do need a Police Attestation Letter (PAL) when applying for a study permit from inside Canada.
